description:
A teammate started a very small proof-of-concept Node project under

/home/user/dev

and left you two things in the sub-directory

/home/user/dev/config

1. project.json – the project’s working manifest  
2. project.schema.json – the JSON-Schema that formally describes what a valid manifest must look like  

Your job is to make sure the manifest is still valid and then produce a tiny, human-readable dependency summary for the rest of the team.

Desired steps (high level – you must choose the concrete shell commands yourself):

• Validate /home/user/dev/config/project.json against /home/user/dev/config/project.schema.json.  
  – If the file is valid, continue.  
  – If the file is *not* valid the task fails, so make absolutely sure validation succeeds before proceeding.  

• Use jq to read the manifest and create the file

/home/user/dev/project_summary.txt

with the exact text format shown below (the automated tests will perform an exact byte-for-byte comparison, including line breaks and white-space):

Line 1:  === Dependency Summary ===  
Line 2+: one line per dependency, sorted **alphabetically (A→Z)** by the dependency name, each line using the arrow token “ -> ” (space-arrow-space) between name and version.

Example layout (do **not** hard-code the values; read them from project.json):

=== Dependency Summary ===  
alpha-lib -> 1.0.0  
zeta-tool -> 2.3.4  

Technical notes & constraints:

1. Only core Linux utilities that exist in a minimal Ubuntu image are guaranteed. You **may** assume jq (already installed) is available.  
2. No root or sudo access. Everything happens inside /home/user.  
3. Produce the summary file exactly at the given absolute path.  
4. Do not delete, rename or move the existing files – just read from them.  
5. When you are finished, /home/user/dev/project_summary.txt must exist and match the exact format described above.

The automated checker will verify two things:

• project.json passes validation against project.schema.json (exit status 0).  
• /home/user/dev/project_summary.txt exists and exactly matches the required text pattern.

Perform however many shell commands you need, but the whole task can be done in roughly 2-3 commands if you are concise.
truth:
Initial files that are present **before** the agent starts:

1. File: /home/user/dev/config/project.schema.json  
Contents (19 bytes, exact):  
{"$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema", "type": "object", "required": ["name", "version", "dependencies"], "properties": {"name": {"type": "string"}, "version": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$"}, "dependencies": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": {"type": "string"}}}, "additionalProperties": false}

2. File: /home/user/dev/config/project.json  
Contents (pretty-printed here for readability – the file in the VM is exactly the same single-line JSON text):  
{
  "name": "sample-poc",
  "version": "0.1.3",
  "dependencies": {
    "express": "4.18.2",
    "lodash": "4.17.21"
  }
}

Target file that **must** be created by the agent:

File: /home/user/dev/project_summary.txt  
Expected contents (including final trailing newline):

=== Dependency Summary ===
express -> 4.18.2
lodash -> 4.17.21

Important: dependencies are sorted alphabetically, and there is a single trailing newline at the end of the file.
name: task_000000_4c51482f
